evals.question_short: Trigger/routing — outline the shadow procedure for a thread URL

assertions.text: Names all 7 steps: pick/validate, spec extraction, shadow implementation, ground-truth fetch, diff+score, skill feedback, aggregate
States the COVER-only / patch-not-read discipline applies until Step 4
Names spec.md as the Step 2 artifact
Names comparison.md as the Step 5 artifact
Names skill-gaps.md as the Step 6 artifact
Names plan.md (Step 3) and notes.md (Step 3 implementation) as artifacts
Outputs land under knowledge/shadow-implementations/<slug>/ (NOT under planning/<slug>/)
Names the M2 context-awareness pre-step (date / author posture / engagement)
Names the M5 engagement classification (unengaged / acked / debated / contested)
Names the M4 REJECT-track decision as a valid early termination
Names the M1 fallback chain for Step 4 ground-truth fetch failures
Notes the design-only variant exists (skip 3.3-3.5 / no dev branch) for small or additive patches
Names the calibration-signal rationale inline (reading patch poisons the diff)

evals.question_short: Grade a 90%-match, one-design-diff, no-invariant shadow run

assertions.text: Names the grade B (not A, not C)
Cites the B-threshold: ≥70% overlap, no missed invariants, 1-2 design choices diverge
Excludes A because A reserves for runs where the only differences are style/minor naming (no design divergence)
Excludes C because C requires 3+ design diffs OR 40-70% overlap
States REJECT-A/B/C does NOT apply because the run implemented, did not reject
Notes the REJECT track and the A-F track are explicitly mutually exclusive in SKILL.md (different shadow output types)
Notes (design-only) tag does NOT apply — that's the M1 fallback marker for unfetchable patches
Names Critic E reviewer-reflex match as a separate field that supports but does not shift the implementation grade
Records the verdict in comparison.md §Verdict and the design diff in §Novel concerns or §Missed concerns
Notes the M-ordinal for the design diff continues the M1-M5 lineage (does not reset)

evals.question_short: Anti-trigger — pg-shadow vs direct pg-feature-plan + pg-implement

assertions.text: Explicitly says do NOT invoke pg-shadow-implement for real upstream-send-ready work
Cites the skill's frontmatter anti-trigger list (real upstream patches / already-committed / non-PG calibration)
Articulates the COVER-only read rule (Step 2 reads COVER + replies, not the .patch)
Articulates the patch-fetch-only-after rule (Step 4 fetches patch only after Step 3 implementation is done)
Names the calibration purpose (Phase E loop / measurement of planner-suite accuracy)
Contrasts: calibration goal = restrict context; real upstream goal = use all context
Names the alternative: pg-feature-brainstorm → pg-feature-plan → pg-implement → pg-patch-review (--self) → patch-submission
Names the additional anti-trigger cases: already-committed threads, multi-thread series, undocumented subsystem
Names pg-shadow as a measurement tool, not a development tool

summary.notes: Stricter assertion set (29 → 32); harder probes around inline-schema availability, calibration-rationale wording, mutual-exclusivity language, M-ordinal continuation. with_skill still saturates at 100% with the iter-1 edits applied. Baseline ~unchanged at 37.5% (the 3 new assertions are precisely the ones the skill carries that baseline cannot infer from name).
